displayName: Institute for the Study of War
text: IDF overnight strikes on Tehran targeted missile production capacity at IRGC central production compounds and fuel facilities. Power substation strikes in western Tehran and Karaj suggest broader infrastructure targeting campaign is expanding beyond weapons sites.
verificationNote: Based on confirmed IDF operational announcements and Iran International reporting on Tehran strikes.

text: NATO Sec-Gen Rutte: 22 nations joining coalition to ensure freedom of navigation through Strait of Hormuz 'as soon as possible.' France's Operation Aspides maritime escort mission with ~12 ships is setting up. Germany and Greece explicitly ruled out military participation.
verificationNote: Rutte statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. France/Germany details from CBS News and Bloomberg reporting.

text: IDF overnight strikes confirmed on 4 IRGC ballistic missile production and fuel facilities in Tehran and Karaj. Power substation hits causing localized blackouts in western Tehran reported across multiple Iranian Telegram channels. Air defense suppression operations also confirmed in the capital.
verificationNote: Multiple corroborating Iranian social media reports. Confirmed by Iran International.

text: Hezbollah rocket strike confirmed in Nahariya (Day 24 morning). 6 wounded per Israeli emergency services. IDF 36th Division launched new targeted operation expanding forward defense area in south Lebanon toward Litani. Approximately 400+ Hezbollah fighters killed since Lebanon ground ops began.
verificationNote: IDF and Israeli emergency services confirmed Nahariya strike and casualties.

text: Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires tonight (~7:44 PM ET). Iran has explicitly rejected it. IRGC doctrine shift to offensive posture now includes Gulf power plants, desalination, and US base energy infrastructure as targets. Analysts tracking whether US executes power plant strikes or issues second ultimatum.
verificationNote: Ultimatum timing confirmed by Axios. IRGC counter-threat confirmed by PBS, NBC, PBS NewsHour.

text: IDF killed Abu Khalil Barji, Radwan Force special ops commander, in Majdal Selm, southern Lebanon via drone strike. Barji was directing cross-border attacks and coordinating Hezbollah-IRGC liaison for the Lebanon front. One of the highest-value Hezbollah targets eliminated since war began.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel, attributed to IDF Spokesperson.

text: Geolocated strike footage from Lebanon confirms Qasmiyeh Bridge on the coastal road and Zrarieh Bridge both destroyed. Two additional Litani crossings under watch. Lebanese President Aoun calling bridge demolitions a prelude to a ground invasion north of the river.
verificationNote: CGTN footage of Qasmiyeh Bridge strike and Al Jazeera reporting confirmed. Zrarieh confirmed by Times of Israel.

text: Turkey FM Fidan conducted active shuttle diplomacy on March 22 — calls with Araghchi (Iran), Egyptian FM, Kallas (EU), and US officials. Turkey positioning as key mediator but Fidan notes a “growing assessment that US and Israel initial positions are drifting apart” which risks prolonging the war.
verificationNote: Turkey FM Fidan statements confirmed by Israel Hayom and TRT World.

text: Trump posted on Truth Social claiming "productive" US-Iran talks and announced a 5-day pause on strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Key detail: Iran's FM Araghchi immediately denied any contact, calling the claim an attempt to mislead oil traders. Iran's Fars News cited a government source confirming "no direct or indirect contact" with the US.
verificationNote: Trump post confirmed across all major outlets. Iran denial confirmed by Araghchi statement and Fars News source.

text: IDF confirmed a "wide-scale wave of strikes" on Tehran in early morning March 23 — targeting IRGC police HQ, Basij militia base, rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ. Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent called the explosions "unprecedented" in scale, especially on the city's eastern side. Separate CENTCOM strike destroyed the Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant used for IRGC drone manufacturing.
verificationNote: IDF formally announced the operation. CENTCOM confirmed Qom plant with satellite imagery.

text: Day 24 civilian casualty summary from Iranian cities: Tabriz — 6 killed, 6 injured (residential buildings). Khorramabad — 1 child killed, body pulled from rubble. Urmia — buildings "flattened," rescue teams searching. Bandar Abbas — 1 IRIB guard killed at state radio transmitter. Total new confirmed civilian deaths in this morning's strikes: at least 8.
verificationNote: Tabriz confirmed by crisis management official Farshi. Khorramabad by Fars News. Bandar Abbas by Anadolu and Ynet.

text: Iran Defense Council issued an escalatory threat to mine the Strait of Hormuz and the entire Persian Gulf if US or Israeli forces attack Iran's coastline or islands. This would be a qualitative escalation beyond the existing effective blockade — mining represents a persistent threat to all shipping, not just selective denial. IRGC also specifically threatened Gulf power plants: "If you hit electricity, we hit electricity."
verificationNote: Iran Defense Council statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel liveblogs.

text: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) with 2,000+ Marines reportedly moving to the region as the US prepares a "weeks-long operation" to force open the Strait of Hormuz — potentially including seizure of Kharg Island (Iran's main oil export terminal). This plan was in motion before Trump's 5-day pause announcement. The pause buys time but the military option is being readied.
verificationNote: USS Tripoli movement confirmed by Times of Israel. Kharg Island plan reported by Israeli officials.

text: Israel blew up the Qasmiyeh coastal highway bridge over the Litani River — the fifth Litani-area bridge struck since March 2. Lebanon President Aoun called it a "dangerous escalation" and "prelude to a ground invasion" north of the Litani. Defense Minister Katz ordered ALL Litani River bridges demolished. Around 150,000 civilians in southern Lebanon face restricted movement.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, and CGTN footage of bridge strike.

text: CTP-ISW Day 24 assessment: Trump's deal-term claims (no nukes, no enrichment, Hormuz jointly controlled) are unverified. Iran's categorical denial follows a pattern — Tehran refused to acknowledge back-channel contacts even when confirmed by third parties. Key question: is there a 'most respected Iranian leader' distinct from Mojtaba Khamenei who has authority to agree these terms? US officials have not confirmed the counterpart's identity.
verificationNote: CTP-ISW pattern assessment based on prior Iranian negotiating behavior. Deal terms unconfirmed by Iran.

text: Aurora Intel: Iran launched its 76th wave of Operation True Promise 4 strikes against Israel on Day 24. Ballistic missile launches from Iran are down 86% from Day 1 levels per Joint Chiefs chairman — but Iran's recent penetration of Israeli air defenses near Dimona (nuclear site) shows its remaining arsenal can still achieve strategic effect. IDF has destroyed ~300 Iranian missile launchers; Iran retains estimated 70-100 functional launchers.
verificationNote: Operation True Promise 4 wave count from Press TV/Tribune India. 86% reduction from CTP-ISW citing Joint Chiefs. Launcher count estimated via ISW/Alma Center.

text: Hormuz Watch: Windward maritime AI reports Hormuz transits down 94.2% since Feb 28. First Iraqi oil supertanker (2M barrels) crossed the strait today with AIS signal off — suggesting Iran's selective permission system is functional. 40,000+ seafarers remain stranded. Trump's 5-day pause reduces likelihood of imminent US naval seizure operation but military planning continues. 22-nation Hormuz coalition still mobilizing.
verificationNote: Windward 94.2% transit drop confirmed by CBS News. Iraqi supertanker crossing confirmed by Bloomberg.

text: ISW analysis: Turkey's FM Hakan Fidan has made 12+ calls in 48 hours to Iranian, Egyptian, US, and EU counterparts on ceasefire mediation. Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey are serving as intermediaries for US-Iran back-channel contact — consistent with Trump's claim that talks occurred. Pakistan previously hosted P5+1 nuclear talks and has established back-channel with both Washington and Tehran. Formal talks in Islamabad within days is possible.
verificationNote: Turkey FM calls confirmed by CNN. Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey intermediary role confirmed by Bloomberg/Axios.

text: OSINT Defender: Kataib Hezbollah claims 27 attacks on US military bases across Iraq and Gulf in 24 hours ending March 23 — highest single-day tempo of the conflict. Islamic Resistance in Iraq simultaneously extended 5-day pause on US Embassy Baghdad attacks. Simultaneous surge and diplomacy suggests militia is using attack tempo as leverage in negotiations, not contradicting them. Iranian proxy coordination pattern consistent with ceasefire positioning.
verificationNote: Kataib Hezbollah embassy pause confirmed by The National. 27 attacks claim from militia's own statements — individual attacks not independently verified.

text: ACLED crisis update: IEA confirms 40+ energy facilities across 9 countries severely damaged — makes this the largest supply disruption in modern oil market history, per IEA. Brent was briefly at $119/barrel before Trump's pause pulled it to $101. If 5-day pause leads to ceasefire talks, markets may drop further. If talks fail, military option including Kharg Island seizure returns to table. Outcome window: March 28.
verificationNote: IEA 40+ facilities statement confirmed by NBC News live updates. Brent $119 peak confirmed by Bloomberg.

text: OSINT Defender: US officials have confirmed at least 12 Iranian naval mines — identified as Maham 3 and Maham 7 Limpet Mine models — are currently positioned in the Strait of Hormuz. These mines are the primary physical obstacle preventing commercial transit beyond the IRGC active deterrence. Removal would require coordinated MCM (mine countermeasures) operation before safe shipping lanes could be declared.
verificationNote: US officials confirmed mine presence to CBS News. Maham-type identification from CENTCOM assessment.

text: GeoConfirmed analysis: THAAD system failure to intercept Iranian missiles at Dimona (March 21-22) is under active investigation. CENTCOM reviewing 3 separate THAAD intercept attempts that missed. This is significant — THAAD was considered the 'last layer' for ballistic missiles penetrating Iron Dome. 180+ injured in Arad and Dimona after the defense failure. Similar failure was observed at Erbil THAAD battery during Day 3.
verificationNote: THAAD failure at Dimona/Arad reported by OSINTdefender. NBC confirmed 180+ injured after air defense penetration. CENTCOM investigation referenced in CBS News.

text: Intel Crab: IRGC expanded its attack envelope today — Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia and US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain both struck with ballistic missiles and drones. Saudi Arabia confirms 2 missiles fired toward Riyadh, 1 intercepted. This marks escalation into GCC territory beyond the Hormuz strategic perimeter. Iran simultaneously denying any talks with US while expanding its target set.
verificationNote: IRGC confirmed the strikes. Saudi confirmation of 2 ballistic missiles toward Riyadh via Al Jazeera.

text: War Monitor: IDF has issued evacuation warnings for four southern Beirut suburbs (Bourj el-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik, Chiyah) following confirmed killing of an IRGC Quds Force member in Beirut. These are the same neighborhoods housing Hezbollah's political bureau and administrative infrastructure. If struck, this would represent the most significant IDF Beirut operation since the start of the current conflict.
verificationNote: IDF evacuation warnings confirmed by JPost and Times of Israel. IRGC member killing confirmed by IDF.

text: Iranian military source via Tasnim: Iran has 'new surprises planned for coming days' that 'could produce very big results.' Source claims Trump 'knows his offensive and defensive military ammunition is in a very bad condition.' Iran simultaneously fired 6 separate missile barrages at Israel on Day 24 — highest single-day launch tempo recorded in the conflict.
verificationNote: Tasnim News article confirmed. Iranian military source claims unverified. Six barrages confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

text: OSINTdefender: Pentagon has made detailed preparations for US special forces to be deployed into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium — plans submitted pending presidential decision. A separate contingency plan for US ground forces in Iran more broadly has also been presented to senior military commanders. Both remain on hold pending outcome of Trump's 5-day pause.
verificationNote: OSINTdefender reporting confirmed by NBC News and CBS News liveblog. Ground force plan details from Pentagon sources to multiple outlets.

text: ACLED: Iran fired 6 separate ballistic missile barrages at Israel on Day 24 — highest single-day launch tempo in the conflict. Day 24 simultaneously saw Trump's 5-day pause announcement, IRGC strikes on Saudi/Bahrain, and Kataib Hezbollah claiming 27 attacks on US bases. Iran is signaling military strength while leaving diplomatic doors open. The 5-day clock runs to March 28.
verificationNote: Six barrages confirmed by Times of Israel and Alma Research Center daily report. 5-day pause confirmed by all major outlets.

text: ISW analysis: Iran's categorical denial of Trump's negotiation claims follows a consistent pattern — Tehran historically refuses to acknowledge back-channel contacts even when confirmed by third parties. Key open question: who is the 'most respected Iranian leader' Trump spoke with? US officials confirm it is NOT Araghchi; preference is for parliament speaker Qalibaf, who has more pragmatic credibility. Pakistan as meeting venue within days is viable.
verificationNote: ISW CTP pattern assessment. Qalibaf preference confirmed by NPR. Pakistan venue reporting by Bloomberg and Axios.

text: War Monitor: Oil markets saw historic intraday swing on Day 24 — Brent hit /barrel in early trading, crashed below  on Trump's ceasefire claim (largest single-day % drop of the war), settled at .44. IEA chief Birol confirmed this is the largest oil supply disruption in modern history. Goldman Sachs warns: if Hormuz flows stay at 5% of normal for 10 weeks, prices will likely exceed the 2008 record of /barrel.
verificationNote: Brent  peak and .44 close confirmed by Bloomberg. IEA Birol statement confirmed. Goldman Sachs forecast from Bloomberg.

text: Intel Crab: Netanyahu reportedly frustrated Mossad's promises of a popular uprising in Iran have not materialized — per NYT citing current and former US/Israeli intelligence officials. Netanyahu has called on world leaders to join the coalition, warning Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM tests prove it has 4,000km+ range reaching deep into Europe. Israel and US remain aligned operationally, but divergences on war's end-state are widening.
verificationNote: Netanyahu frustration with Mossad uprising prediction confirmed by Times of Israel. Diego Garcia capability warning confirmed by Jerusalem Post and NBC.
